title: SSRF, DNS Rebinding, Injection, Response Validation, and Least Authority · Go Client Systems · Execution Trace: Request Field Injection
summary: An image-import service accepts tenant URLs and must stop SSRF, DNS rebinding, unsafe redirects, hostile content, and secret-bearing logs. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go request field injection increment in a Go 1.26 net/http defense-in-depth outbound request policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reject control characters and unsafe dynamic structure in targets and fields and rely on maintained encoders. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Address DNS rebinding and validation-to-connect races by binding policy to resolved and connected destinations. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Request Field Injection · Theory Request Field Injection
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model receives https://127.0.0.1:8005/meta, a decimal IP alias, a hostname that rebinds, and a redirect to file://. Enforce scheme, resolution, connected-address, redirect, response, egress, and privacy defenses. Competency probe: Reject control characters and unsafe dynamic structure in targets and fields and rely on maintained encoders.
